title: The 2022 Movie Star Ranking: 35 Under 35, Revisited
format: Sean and Amanda revisit their 35-under-35 movie star ranking a second time, prompted by new Jennifer Lawrence and Harry Styles releases, with producer Bobby Wagner weighing in throughout.

segments.heading: The state of movie stardom
segments.note: two under-35 streaming releases prompted the rewatch

films.title: Causeway
films.note: Jennifer Lawrence's best work in a long time, per Sean; a quiet two-hander with Brian Tyree Henry.

films.title: My Policeman
films.note: Harry Styles, better here than in Don't Worry Darling, in a 1960s repression triangle.

films.title: Don't Worry Darling
films.note: The comparison point for how far Styles has come as an actor.

films.title: Lady Chatterley's Lover
films.note: Emma Corrin's next lead; she didn't make the cut this year.

segments.heading: Aged out, cut, and just missed
segments.note: who fell off the list, who got cut, and who's next in line

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Miles Teller, aged out; would've been near the top after this.

films.title: Ticket to Paradise
films.note: Kaitlyn Dever, cut; the Clooney/Roberts marketing did the work, not her.

films.title: Bullet Train
films.note: Joey King, cut; slightly miscast, by the hosts' own admission.

films.title: In the Heights
films.note: Anthony Ramos, cut; hasn't had a film since.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Awkwafina, cut; still her high point.

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: Awkwafina, cut; the fourth-lead comic-relief role that stalled her.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: Elizabeth Olsen, cut; spoiler alert for Wanda's fate.

films.title: Whitney Houston: I Wanna Dance with Somebody
films.note: Naomi Ackie, next in line, playing Whitney Houston.

films.title: Blink Twice
films.note: Naomi Ackie and Adria Arjona both cast in Zoe Kravitz's directing debut, then still titled Pussy Island.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: Naomi Ackie's underused Jannah.

films.title: Deep Water
films.note: Jacob Elordi, next in line, as the hotel piano player.

films.title: The Kissing Booth
films.note: Jacob Elordi's other, much bigger, audience.

films.title: Saltburn
films.note: Jacob Elordi, forthcoming, opposite Barry Keoghan.

films.title: Priscilla
films.note: Jacob Elordi as Elvis, in Sofia Coppola's film.

films.title: The Woman King
films.note: Thuso Mbedu, next in line, as the younger lead.

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: The comp the hosts use for whether The Woman King can contend for Best Picture.

films.title: Bodies Bodies Bodies
films.note: Amandla Stenberg, next in line; the heartbeat of a divisive movie.

films.title: Dear Evan Hansen
films.note: Amandla Stenberg; a bad character, a good performance.

films.title: The Hate U Give
films.note: Amandla Stenberg, very good.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Mike Faist, next in line.

films.title: Challengers
films.note: Mike Faist's next film, Luca Guadagnino's tennis triangle.

films.title: Barbarian
films.note: Bill Skarsgard, a sneaky breakout hit the hosts feel bad about cutting.

films.title: It
films.note: Bill Skarsgard's Pennywise.

films.title: Nosferatu
films.note: Bill Skarsgard, likely cast in the Robert Eggers version.

films.title: Where the Crawdads Sing
films.note: Daisy Edgar-Jones, cut; 'possibly never seen anyone more miscast,' per Amanda, and it still made $150M.

films.title: Fresh
films.note: Daisy Edgar-Jones, very good in this one.

films.title: Triple Frontier
films.note: Adria Arjona, argued for and lost; Sean's pick.

films.title: 6 Underground
films.note: Adria Arjona.

films.title: Father of the Bride
films.note: Adria Arjona, better than the movie deserved.

films.title: Morbius
films.note: Adria Arjona's forgotten role as Martine Bancroft.

films.title: Hit Man
films.note: Adria Arjona, forthcoming, opposite Glen Powell.

films.title: Blink Twice
films.note: Adria Arjona, forthcoming, then still titled Pussy Island.

films.title: Being Mortal
films.note: Keke Palmer's shelved Aziz Ansari film, in limbo after the Bill Murray conduct allegations.

films.title: Merrily We Roll Along
films.note: Beanie Feldstein, cut; filming Richard Linklater's decades-long production, her only project of note at the moment.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: No. 35 Harry Styles: part of a CV that's 'not the worst I've ever seen.'

films.title: Eternals
films.note: No. 35 Harry Styles: his Marvel stinger cameo as Eros.

films.title: Don't Worry Darling
films.note: No. 35 Harry Styles: made $85M, largely on his fan base.

films.title: My Policeman
films.note: No. 35 Harry Styles: this week's release, better than expected.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: No. 34 Rachel Zegler: one of Sean's favorite movies of last year.

films.title: Shazam! Fury of the Gods
films.note: No. 34 Rachel Zegler, forthcoming.

films.title: Snow White
films.note: No. 34 Rachel Zegler, cast as Disney's live-action lead.

films.title: The Hunger Games: The Ballad of Songbirds & Snakes
films.note: No. 34 Rachel Zegler, cast in a central role.

films.title: The Lost Daughter
films.note: No. 33 Paul Mescal: a smaller, less-loved role.

films.title: Carmen
films.note: No. 33 Paul Mescal, in Benjamin Millepied's dance update of the Bizet opera.

films.title: All of Us Strangers
films.note: No. 33 Paul Mescal, opposite Claire Foy and Andrew Scott.

films.title: Foe
films.note: No. 33 Paul Mescal, adapting the Iain Reid novel.

films.title: Scream
films.note: No. 32 Jenna Ortega, picked over Adria Arjona in the hosts' one real fight; the new Neve Campbell of the franchise.

films.title: X
films.note: No. 32 Jenna Ortega.

films.title: The Fallout
films.note: No. 32 Jenna Ortega: her best performance, per Sean.

films.title: The Woman King
films.note: No. 31 Lashana Lynch: a supporting part that jumps off the screen.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: No. 31 Lashana Lynch.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: No. 31 Lashana Lynch, in the Bond franchise.

films.title: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical
films.note: No. 31 Lashana Lynch as Miss Honey.

films.title: Bob Marley: One Love
films.note: No. 31 Lashana Lynch, cast as Rita Marley.

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: No. 30 Stephanie Hsu: in the Best Supporting Actress conversation, alongside Michelle Yeoh.

films.title: The Fall Guy
films.note: No. 30 Stephanie Hsu, forthcoming, opposite Ryan Gosling.

films.title: Stars at Noon
films.note: No. 29 Joe Alwyn: the mysterious British operator.

films.title: The Souvenir Part II
films.note: No. 29 Joe Alwyn.

films.title: Kinds of Kindness
films.note: No. 29 Joe Alwyn, then known as End (Yorgos Lanthimos's other movie), opposite Emma Stone.

films.title: X
films.note: No. 28 Mia Goth.

films.title: Pearl
films.note: No. 28 Mia Goth: one of the best performances of the year, per Sean.

films.title: High Life
films.note: No. 28 Mia Goth.

films.title: Emma.
films.note: No. 28 Mia Goth.

films.title: Infinity Pool
films.note: No. 28 Mia Goth, forthcoming, from Brandon Cronenberg.

films.title: MaXXXine
films.note: No. 28 Mia Goth, closing out the X trilogy.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: No. 27 Letitia Wright.

films.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
films.note: No. 27 Letitia Wright, out this week, presumed to center on her.

films.title: Death on the Nile
films.note: No. 27 Letitia Wright, a film Sean was 'angry' about.

films.title: Cha Cha Real Smooth
films.note: No. 26 Dakota Johnson, in one of the year's weirder roles.

films.title: Persuasion
films.note: No. 26 Dakota Johnson: 'really, really offensive,' per Amanda.

films.title: Madame Web
films.note: No. 26 Dakota Johnson, cast as the Spider-Man-universe clairvoyant.

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: No. 25 Simu Liu: moved up nine spots on the strength of this.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: No. 25 Simu Liu, forthcoming.

films.title: Arthur the King
films.note: No. 25 Simu Liu, forthcoming.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: No. 24 Ana de Armas: arguably the best part of it.

films.title: Deep Water
films.note: No. 24 Ana de Armas: 'a gift to me,' per Sean, who liked it alone.

films.title: The Gray Man
films.note: No. 24 Ana de Armas: not the worst part, for once.

films.title: Blonde
films.note: No. 24 Ana de Armas: one of the year's least favorite movies, not her fault.

films.title: Ghosted
films.note: No. 24 Ana de Armas, forthcoming, opposite Chris Evans.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: No. 23 Brie Larson: her biggest franchise.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: No. 23 Brie Larson: her last movie, three years ago.

films.title: The Marvels
films.note: No. 23 Brie Larson, forthcoming.

films.title: Fast X
films.note: No. 23 Brie Larson, forthcoming: 'plug and play' casting, per Amanda.

films.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
films.note: No. 22 Barry Keoghan, opposite Colin Farrell.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: No. 22 Barry Keoghan.

films.title: The Green Knight
films.note: No. 22 Barry Keoghan.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: No. 22 Barry Keoghan, probably the best role in it.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: No. 22 Barry Keoghan, as the Joker, barely seen or heard.

films.title: The Banshees of Inisherin
films.note: No. 22 Barry Keoghan: the fourth lead who leaves the biggest impression.

films.title: Saltburn
films.note: No. 22 Barry Keoghan, forthcoming, from Emerald Fennell.

films.title: I'm Thinking of Ending Things
films.note: No. 21 Jessie Buckley.

films.title: The Lost Daughter
films.note: No. 21 Jessie Buckley: an Oscar nomination last year.

films.title: Women Talking
films.note: No. 21 Jessie Buckley: 'tenacious,' per Sean, in a challenging part.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: No. 20 Austin Butler.

films.title: Elvis
films.note: No. 20 Austin Butler: 'he has it,' likely headed for an Oscar nomination.

films.title: Dune: Part Two
films.note: No. 20 Austin Butler, forthcoming, as Feyd-Rautha.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: No. 19 Saoirse Ronan.

films.title: Brooklyn
films.note: No. 19 Saoirse Ronan.

films.title: The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun
films.note: No. 19 Saoirse Ronan, wonderful in about 20 minutes of it.

films.title: See How They Run
films.note: No. 19 Saoirse Ronan: a slightly soft Agatha Christie parody.

films.title: Foe
films.note: No. 19 Saoirse Ronan, forthcoming.

films.title: Hustlers
films.note: No. 18 Keke Palmer, in a smaller part.

films.title: Nope
films.note: No. 18 Keke Palmer: Jordan Peele put her at the center; 'the sweetness to Kaluuya's saltiness.'

films.title: Lightyear
films.note: No. 18 Keke Palmer, voice work; a hit with actual kids, per Sean's vacation story.

films.title: Monkey Man
films.note: No. 17 Dev Patel, forthcoming: his directing and starring debut.

films.title: The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar
films.note: No. 17 Dev Patel, forthcoming, from Wes Anderson.

films.title: Pieces of a Woman
films.note: No. 16 Vanessa Kirby: an Oscar nomination in 2021.

films.title: The Son
films.note: No. 16 Vanessa Kirby.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One
films.note: No. 16 Vanessa Kirby, forthcoming.

films.title: Napoleon
films.note: No. 16 Vanessa Kirby, forthcoming, replacing Jodie Comer as Josephine.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: No. 15 Glen Powell: finally the big one, as Hangman.

films.title: Apollo 10½: A Space Age Childhood
films.note: No. 15 Glen Powell, voice work.

films.title: Devotion
films.note: No. 15 Glen Powell, opposite Jonathan Majors.

films.title: Hit Man
films.note: No. 15 Glen Powell, forthcoming, with Richard Linklater.

films.title: Sorry to Bother You
films.note: No. 14 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: No. 14 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: No. 14 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: No. 14 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: No. 14 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Haunted Mansion
films.note: No. 14 LaKeith Stanfield, forthcoming: the Disney ride adaptation.

films.title: The Power of the Dog
films.note: No. 13 Jesse Plemons.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: No. 13 Jesse Plemons, forthcoming, reportedly took over the lead from Leonardo DiCaprio.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: No. 12 Jennifer Lawrence, kind of easy to forget she was in it.

films.title: Causeway
films.note: No. 12 Jennifer Lawrence: her best work in a long time, per Sean.

films.title: No Hard Feelings
films.note: No. 12 Jennifer Lawrence, forthcoming: a Craigslist-ad comedy with 'super bad-ish vibes.'

films.title: Spencer
films.note: No. 11 Kristen Stewart: not well received.

films.title: Crimes of the Future
films.note: No. 11 Kristen Stewart: 'downright hilarious' as the third or fourth lead.

films.title: The Last Black Man in San Francisco
films.note: No. 10 Jonathan Majors.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: No. 10 Jonathan Majors.

films.title: The Harder They Fall
films.note: No. 10 Jonathan Majors.

films.title: Devotion
films.note: No. 10 Jonathan Majors, opposite Glen Powell.

films.title: Creed III
films.note: No. 10 Jonathan Majors, forthcoming, as the villain.

films.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
films.note: No. 10 Jonathan Majors, forthcoming, kicking off his run as Kang.

films.title: Kimi
films.note: No. 9 Zoe Kravitz, in a Soderbergh update of Klute.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: No. 9 Zoe Kravitz, a great Catwoman.

films.title: Blink Twice
films.note: No. 9 Zoe Kravitz's writing-directing debut, then still titled Pussy Island.

films.title: Last Night in Soho
films.note: No. 8 Anya Taylor-Joy: 'magnificent,' per Sean.

films.note: No. 8 Anya Taylor-Joy: a top-five scene of the year, per Sean.

films.title: Amsterdam
films.note: No. 8 Anya Taylor-Joy.

films.title: The Menu
films.note: No. 8 Anya Taylor-Joy, truly the star, alongside Ralph Fiennes.

films.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
films.note: No. 8 Anya Taylor-Joy, forthcoming, voicing Princess Peach.

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: No. 8 Anya Taylor-Joy, forthcoming, as the title character.

films.title: Kinds of Kindness

films.title: Don't Worry Darling

films.note: No. 5 Tom Holland.

films.note: No. 5 Tom Holland.

films.note: No. 5 Tom Holland, not a great run of picks lately.

films.note: No. 2 Timothee Chalamet: the star of it, and 'everybody loved it.'

films.title: Wonka

films.note: No. 1 Margot Robbie: 'one of the most baffling experiences of my moviegoing life,' and still she was good in it.

films.note: No. 1 Margot Robbie, as Harley Quinn.
